title: After Hours

jokes.text: Oscar thinks having a dog is just like having a baby. News flash, if you didn't carry it around in your belly for nine months, it isn't your kid.
jokes.setup: Oscar comparing his dog to a baby
jokes.punchline: Angela's dismissive biological gatekeeping
jokes.explanation: Classic Angela superiority complex, well-structured with good character voice

jokes.text: Exactly. Unless you adopted, of course. That is where we disagree.
jokes.punchline: Oscar's polite but firm correction about adoption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar response - overly diplomatic while making a solid point

jokes.text: Hey, are your little dudes crawling yet? No, three-month-old humans don't do that.
jokes.punchline: Pam's flat correction of Angela's unrealistic expectations
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reality check on Angela's competitive parenting

jokes.text: My Philip is crawling. - Angela is such a liar. - It's maddening.
jokes.setup: Angela claiming her baby is crawling at 3 months
jokes.punchline: Jim and Pam's talking head reactions
jokes.explanation: Classic mockumentary beat - characters calling out obvious lies

jokes.text: Exactly. That's just like crate training.
jokes.punchline: Oscar comparing human babies to dog training
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reinforces Oscar's pet-baby equivalence theme

jokes.text: Like her genes are so important. The world just needs more Pam-Jim DNA. - Thank you, no. - No, thank you.
jokes.setup: Angela criticizing Pam having multiple children
jokes.punchline: Jim and Pam's awkward polite responses to the insult
jokes.explanation: Great cringe moment - Angela's meanness met with Jim/Pam's uncomfortable politeness

jokes.text: There's nothing harder than taking care of a boat. Am I right? Angela and Pam: Unbelievable! Un-be-lie-va-ble.
jokes.setup: Oscar comparing boat ownership to baby care
jokes.punchline: Angela and Pam's unified outrage
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - Oscar's tone-deaf comparison finally unites the feuding mothers

jokes.text: It is the end of the day. It is the end of the week. We managed to fill 40 hours somehow.
jokes.punchline: 'Somehow' - admitting they accomplished nothing
jokes.explanation: Relatable workplace humor about wasting time

jokes.text: Right now, you're like, 'oh, what's more important? Dwight's question, figure out who's the VP? Dwight, VP. Dwight, VP.' Dwight, VP. Dwight, VP...
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to get Andy's attention
jokes.punchline: Repetitive chanting attempting hypnosis
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - overthinking a simple request with pseudo-psychology

jokes.text: I can't be hypnotized, Dwight. I tried it, I ended up smoking more.
jokes.punchline: Andy's failed hypnosis backstory
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection - unexpected personal detail that's both specific and believable for Andy

jokes.text: Like stacking and sorting, or-- Catching butterflies. That's a hobby unless it's for food.
jokes.setup: Dwight suggesting menial tasks for eliminated VP candidates
jokes.punchline: Butterfly catching qualification based on consumption
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - absurd but internally consistent worldview

jokes.text: - Theater. - Waste of time. - Dragging. - That's just a verb. Dragging sticks.
jokes.punchline: Dwight's literal interpretation leading to 'dragging sticks'
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay escalation showing Dwight's concrete thinking

jokes.text: English peoples' main use today is judging American talent. [British accent] You're crap. You're wonderful. They're-- they're mean, but they're incisive.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific commentary on British judges
jokes.explanation: Random but accurate observation about reality TV culture

jokes.text: I'm not allowed to say it's mandatory, so let's just call it 'compulsory.'
jokes.punchline: Using synonym to avoid 'mandatory'
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - perfect manager-speak absurdity in few words

jokes.text: Maybe we'll see the real 'Talla-nasty' we've been hearing so much about. - 'Talla-nasty,' very clever. - Thank you. Wait, wait, you think she invented 'Talla-nasty'?
jokes.punchline: Dwight's incredulity at Jim crediting her for common nickname
jokes.explanation: Good cringe beat - Jim being polite while Dwight calls out the obviousness

jokes.text: I thought I was gonna be hanging out with Stanley on this trip, but he's turned out to be kind of a loose cannon.
jokes.punchline: Stanley as 'loose cannon' - opposite of his usual character
jokes.explanation: Good character inversion - Stanley being wild is inherently funny

jokes.text: Yes, the conference room is set up. I've got pens, I've got paper, I've got a whiteboard. - We are good to go! - Say what? We are good to go! - Say what? - We're good to go! - Stop it. Stop it. - Stop. - Say-- - Andy.
jokes.punchline: Forced call-and-response that everyone shuts down
jokes.explanation: Classic Andy try-hard moment that gets shut down - good cringe but predictable

jokes.text: Ball droppings can be beautiful. For example, when it turns an awkward soprano into a rich, full tenor.
jokes.setup: Andy mentioning someone dropped the ball
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding leads to puberty reference
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay that fits Andy's tendency toward awkward tangents

jokes.text: Genghis Khan could take 'em both down 'cause he's not afraid to kill children. What? It's true. He-- He would-- He would eviscerate babies and hang them from poles outside of the villages.
jokes.punchline: Ryan's disturbing historical escalation
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation that's shocking and perfectly in character for Ryan's try-hard intellectualism

jokes.text: Okay, everyone empty your pockets. Whoever has the most seeds is the king, whoever has the least buttons is the hunchback. What happens if you're the hunchback? - Oh, you get kicked.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre arbitrary rules ending in violence
jokes.explanation: Excellently absurd Dwight logic - completely arbitrary rules that somehow make sense to him

jokes.text: 40. Always. - Can I see? - Sure. So I get to kick you now. No, they're not transferable just 'cause I handed 'em to you. Well, that's how I played it in college. - College rules. [Kicks] - Aah!
jokes.punchline: Todd exploiting loophole to kick Dwight
jokes.explanation: Good physical payoff to the absurd game setup

jokes.text: And he lost at his own lame game. Ooh, double meaning. The game was lame, and now he's lame from the kick. Quick wit.
jokes.punchline: Todd explaining his own double meaning
jokes.explanation: Weak - explaining your own wordplay kills the joke

jokes.text: I'll have a glass of your oakiest chardonnay, please. And I will have a waffle with your mapliest syrup.
jokes.punchline: Made-up superlatives for food ordering
jokes.explanation: Cute wordplay that fits the characters' quirky speech patterns

jokes.text: I'm young, and I can. And if I can't, I'm still pretty young. I guess I'll always be young.
jokes.punchline: Circular logic about always being young
jokes.explanation: Perfect Erin logic - sweet but fundamentally confused about how aging works

jokes.text: Why would Erin confide in me that she's staying here in Florida if she didn't want me to make some sort of last-ditch, nothing-to-lose, Kelly ka-who run at her?
jokes.punchline: Made-up phrase 'Kelly ka-who run'
jokes.explanation: Decent Andy rambling with made-up phrase, but lacks punch

jokes.text: - Paid. - I'm dying! - Processed. - And delivered.
jokes.punchline: Kevin's dramatic outburst about boredom
jokes.explanation: Good Kevin moment - his childlike response to mundane work

jokes.text: Sometimes I get so bored I just want to scream, and then sometimes I actually do scream. I just sort of feel out what the situation calls for.
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact description of screaming at work
jokes.explanation: Great Kevin logic - treating workplace screaming as a reasonable response

jokes.text: The cowgirl has chosen her saddle. [Clicks tongue, whinnies]
jokes.setup: Todd claiming Nellie wants him for VP
jokes.punchline: Awkward horse metaphor with sound effects
jokes.explanation: Appropriately cringey but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Well, saddles... [Clicks tongue, whinnies] Sometimes fall off, especially if you don't properly cinch the girth.
jokes.punchline: Dwight extending metaphor with technical horse knowledge
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - taking a bad metaphor and making it technically accurate

jokes.text: I went south of the border for this. And then I went south of that border. And we have ourselves... [Jamaican accent] A Jamaican feast, man.
jokes.punchline: Geographic confusion leading to Jamaica
jokes.explanation: Decent setup-punchline with Andy's typical geographic confusion

jokes.text: If I wanted Jamaican food, I'd just hire a bunch of bodyguards and go there.
jokes.punchline: Ryan's privileged assumption about travel danger
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ryan - casual mention of extreme wealth and paranoia

jokes.text: Don't play dumb with me. I know what's up. Honey, I am not sleeping with-- Hey! Hey... Let's get back to the food. Brandon tell us about these delicious-looking goops.
jokes.punchline: Andy's desperate redirect to 'delicious-looking goops'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe management from Andy with unappetizing food description

jokes.text: - Blow in my ear. - All right. Like I'm on the beach. - [Blows] - Oh. [Imitates seagull calls] Seagull. [Cawing] And-- and a wave crashing.
jokes.punchline: Escalating ridiculous sound effects roleplay
jokes.explanation: Basic cringe comedy but lacks cleverness

jokes.text: Is this March Madness? I love March Madness. Oh, no, that's not this 'cause that's in March.
jokes.setup: Cathy trying to connect with Jim over basketball
jokes.punchline: Realizing her logical error immediately
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective 'trying too hard' moment

jokes.text: - Lebron James. - Yes, nice. Good word association. [Giggles]
jokes.punchline: Jim's patronizing response to basic knowledge
jokes.explanation: Good awkward beat - Jim being politely condescending

jokes.text: Impressive. Oh, please, anyone can tie a knot. The real skill is in untying it. Mm...
jokes.punchline: Sexual innuendo about untying
jokes.explanation: Predictable innuendo without much craft

jokes.text: Like a hospital needs more napkins for surgery--
jokes.punchline: Bizarre hospital napkin emergency example
jokes.explanation: Great Kelly logic - completely wrong but delivered with confidence

jokes.text: Something interesting is happening here for once in my life. I am staying here.
jokes.setup: Kelly being asked to leave drama
jokes.punchline: Admitting her life is usually boring
jokes.explanation: Good Kelly honesty about her desperate need for drama

jokes.text: Careful, Jim. It gets easier and easier. - No, no, no, Stanley. - Mm-hmm. - Stanley. - Uh-huh. Stanley. All right, hey. You know what? Just bring back those bottles!
jokes.punchline: Jim's panicked overreaction and Stanley's knowing responses
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment - Stanley's worldly cynicism vs Jim's panic

jokes.text: Yeah, they keep them on 'cause it's less expensive than turning them on and off. I like how guys just know stuff all the time.
jokes.punchline: Erin's innocent admiration of male 'knowledge'
jokes.explanation: Sweet Erin moment - genuinely impressed by mundane 'facts'

jokes.text: [Whispers] Get down. It's the fuzz. Shh! Do you want us to get scolded? Shut your beautiful, beautiful mouth, please.
jokes.setup: Andy and Erin making waffles illegally
jokes.punchline: Over-dramatic reaction to hotel staff plus awkward compliment
jokes.explanation: Good Andy escalation - treating minor rule-breaking like a heist

jokes.text: Okay, 'cause three dots means 'to be continued,' four dots is a typo, but five dots means, 'whoa, do not make me say what I want to say, baby, but if I did, it would blow your mind.' Dot, dot, dot, dot, dot.
jokes.punchline: Kelly's elaborate punctuation theory
jokes.explanation: Excellent Kelly moment - absurd but confidently delivered expertise on text punctuation

jokes.text: You look like Ed Harris. If they stretched him a little bit.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific celebrity comparison with qualifier
jokes.explanation: Good specific insult with unexpected celebrity reference

jokes.text: Our biggest attraction is our 200-year-old mattresses.
jokes.setup: Dwight worried about bedbugs at Schrute Farms
jokes.punchline: Ancient mattresses as tourist attraction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight business logic - highlighting the worst feature as an attraction

jokes.text: Describe its mood. Did it seem sleepy? Stressed, but to be fair, it was a tense situation.
jokes.punchline: Jim playing along with mood assessment
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist exchange - Dwight's serious bug psychology met by Jim's cooperation

jokes.text: So smug. Like he thought it was funny, like this. - Pssh. That's a bedbug. - Yeah. - Everything's a joke. - I know.
jokes.punchline: Both getting genuinely annoyed at imaginary bug's smugness
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - both characters fully invested in hating this fictional bug

jokes.text: When I jump into the bed, you are gonna cover me with the sheet immediately. And then we'll see who's laughing.
jokes.punchline: Treating himself as human bait with dramatic flair
jokes.explanation: Good Dwight over-engineering of simple problem

jokes.text: Oh, it's plenty warm, and I was farting continuously under the sheet, creating a kind of greenhouse effect.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his bedbug trap conditions
jokes.punchline: Gross scientific description of his method
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight - oversharing disgusting details as scientific method

jokes.text: Packer is trying to bed Nellie for the VP job unless I do it first. Is that really how you want to get this job? Such a chorus girl.
jokes.punchline: Calling Jim 'chorus girl' for questioning ethics
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Dwight's warped masculinity views

jokes.text: A real man swallows his vomit when a lady is present.
jokes.setup: Todd vomiting in front of Nellie
jokes.punchline: Bizarre chivalry rule
jokes.explanation: Excellent gross-out line that's both disgusting and absurdly specific

jokes.text: [Giggles] Wait, this is why I exercise like a fiend. Feel. Seriously.
jokes.setup: Cathy making a move on Jim
jokes.punchline: Obvious attempt to get physical contact
jokes.explanation: Basic seduction attempt - creates cringe but lacks wit

jokes.text: Oh, my God! What are you thinking? Um... I mean-- I know that you're married. I sat at your wife's desk. How little do you think of me?
jokes.punchline: Cathy's fake outrage and guilt trip
jokes.explanation: Good manipulation tactic - turning rejection into offense

jokes.text: My friend Jim would tell you to play it cool. My husband would tell you to go for it.
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging Jim's different perspectives as friend vs husband
jokes.explanation: Excellent insight into relationship dynamics with perfect economy

jokes.text: We could get a dog. We could go to R-rated movies. And who knows, I mean, you're a guy, I'm a girl-- Yeah? - We could be roommates. - Really? - Maybe in six months-- Six months?
jokes.punchline: Andy's disappointed reaction to six-month timeline
jokes.explanation: Good romantic comedy beat - different timeline expectations

jokes.text: Um, okay, I'm in love with Kelly.
jokes.punchline: Ryan's abrupt confession about Kelly
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Ryan interrupting wrong conversation

jokes.text: Seven, one, one, nine... No, no, no. Not numbers, no. Try again. Ugh, I'm still getting numbers. Seven, one, one-- Is anyone around here thinking the numbers seven, one, one?
jokes.punchline: Dwight getting wrong numbers, room number setup
jokes.explanation: Good setup for room number reveal with Dwight's literal thinking

jokes.text: Win at all costs. Don't respect women. These are the tenets I was brought up with, and they have served me well. But my ancestors never worked in corporate America. They were farmers. And before that, hunters. And before that, time travelers. And before that, me again.
jokes.punchline: Ancestry escalating to time travel and circular logic
jokes.explanation: Excellent Dwight monologue - perfectly escalated absurdity with circular time travel logic

jokes.text: I got Romy And Michele's High School Reunion.
jokes.punchline: Oddly specific 90s movie choice
jokes.explanation: Mild Andy quirk but not particularly funny

jokes.text: Hey, just so you know, me and you-- I don't think that's ridiculous. Dot, dot, dot... Dot, dot.
jokes.setup: Darryl talking to Val after text analysis
jokes.punchline: Using Kelly's dot theory for flirtation
jokes.explanation: Good callback application - Darryl using Kelly's system

jokes.text: Stand back! Aah! Stop it, oh, my God! That burns! What is that? It's a compound of chemicals I pulled off of the maid's cart.
jokes.punchline: Improvised toxic chemical spray
jokes.explanation: Good physical escalation of bedbug hunt

jokes.text: You can't stay here. This place is a biohazard. If I were you, I'd just bunk with Cathy.
jokes.punchline: Dwight unknowingly suggesting exactly what Cathy wants
jokes.explanation: Good ironic suggestion that makes Jim's situation worse

jokes.text: Look, the light went off. Just as I said the light went on.
jokes.setup: Nellie trying to get into Dwight's room
jokes.punchline: Confidently wrong about light timing
jokes.explanation: Mild Nellie confusion but not particularly strong

tmdb_overview: A power struggle erupts when Dwight and Todd Packer compete for a vice president position under Nellie.
